currentImplementationDrift: src/types.ts currently defines MorBoundaryId as seven categories, not universal + eight scoped affinities.
src/config/categories.ts says universalism is encoded as low across all seven boundaries plus high intensity. The new model says universal is an explicit baseline, not absence of boundaries.
sexual is currently folded into gender. The new model needs sexual as its own scoped affinity if kept conceptually distinct.
political_tribe naming should be normalized to political_camp or another canonical term before new work starts.
Question evidence maps currently use MOR, PF, TRB, and TRB_ANCHOR. None directly emit universalAffinity, scopedAffinity, or excessAffinity.

proposed_model.universalAffinity: baseline moral concern for humans in general, explicit scalar in [0,1]

scopedAffinities.national: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.religious: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.ethnic_racial: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.class: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.gender: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.sexual: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.ideological: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]
scopedAffinities.political_camp: affinity toward this narrower group in [0,1]

proposed_model.activeBoundaryRule: group is active only when excessAffinity[group] > 0

evidence.note: domestic vs abroad lives; strongest existing universal-vs-national moral-scope item
equal standing inside polity; good for within-polity universal floor but not all-human baseline
directly names moral scope but asks issue priority rather than affinity
MOR pole battery; useful but legacy continuous-node framing
military intervention scope; confounded with realism/security
international engagement; confounded with institutions and strategy
planet/future and fairness options imply broad scope but not direct affinity
veil-of-ignorance item implies impartiality, not affinity magnitude
global_citizen option is identity/scope cue, not universal moral floor

universal.gap: No clean direct universalAffinity item asks baseline concern for any human stranger. Existing MOR items are policy-scope or issue-salience proxies.

evidence.note: immigration-national-identity salience slider
national identity ranking
national membership criteria priority sort
patriotism/institutional trust, mixes pride with trust
international engagement with home-focus option
trade nationalism, confounded with economics
national_strength item
national strength option

national.gap: Good existing evidence, but it measures identity importance and membership criteria more than affinity above universal.

evidence.note: religion in upbringing; membership/context not affinity
religious identity ranking
religious heritage as national belonging criterion
religion in public life; position and epistemic cross-load
community/tradition items can imply religious boundary but not direct

religious.gap: Has membership/background and public-life items; lacks a clean 'my religious in-group receives extra moral weight' item.

evidence.note: ethnic/racial identity ranking
admissions approach; issue proxy not own-group affinity
discrimination attribution; structural view not affinity
universal equal standing can constrain ethnic/racial excess but does not identify own-group affinity

ethnic_racial.gap: Only one strong direct identity-priority item. Direct own-group affinity is missing; most existing evidence is policy/structural proxy.

evidence.note: class identity ranking
inequality cause allocation
CEO/worker pay ratio
welfare error tradeoff
factory-closure causes
preferred top marginal tax rate
economic policy priority, not class affinity

class.gap: Several policy/deservingness proxies plus one identity-priority item. Direct class in-group affinity is missing.

evidence.note: gender identity ranking
social-direction priority mentions gender identity but not gender-group affinity

gender.gap: Sparse. Direct identity-priority exists, but no direct gender in-group affinity above universal.

evidence.note: sexual identity ranking
social-direction priority mentions LGBTQ rights but not sexual-group affinity

sexual.gap: Sparse and currently folded into gender in ADR-006. Needs separate category if the new architecture keeps sexual distinct.

evidence.note: ideological identity ranking
how many groups want best/share values
opponent model allocation
individual freedom/fairness/tradition values anchors
cause loyalty can separate ideology from party
cross-party closeness tolerance implies ideological/camp boundary
party ID labels may proxy ideological camp but should not define it

ideological.gap: Moderate evidence from identity ranking, opponent modeling, and cause-vs-party items. Needs cleaner separation from political_camp.

evidence.note: party identification label; label only, not intensity
politics identity centrality; intensity not direction
opponents matter to identity
close friends voted differently
party/cause loyalty
cross-camp family marriage reaction
political thought frequency; intensity proxy
group solidarity feeling, ambiguous group object
priority sort over relationship closeness
patriotism/trust may cross-load with camp

political_camp.gap: Strongest existing coverage, but much of it measures heat/intensity rather than affinity direction. Must not dominate the whole moral-circle construct.
